title: Rei joining school translation question
wikitext:
[]: Rei joining school translation question             Quote Post by V » Sun Apr 29, 2007 10:25 pm in the English dub in episode 5, Toji says Rei has been in school with them "since Seventh Grade". However, I have seen subs that translated this as "First Grade"; which is it?

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sun Apr 29, 2007 10:43 pm Hmm, I will have to go back and check to confirm this myself.

[]:             Quote Post by Tokpile Quohog » Mon Apr 30, 2007 12:26 am I think it was meant to be 1st year which means she's been at that school after finishing grade school (which I doubt she went to). So currently she'd be a 2nd year at their school or something like that.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Apr 30, 2007 12:30 am I'm kind of weak with Japanese, but the only number I can pull out of that sentence is "ichi" or "one", not "nana" or "seven". The ADV version says 7th, but literal translations I've seen says "first". It's a grade level difference.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Apr 30, 2007 12:39 am wait so he means "first year"...at junior high school. Hmm...I think it more likely that she's only been there a year and stuff I guess, but I like to check.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Apr 30, 2007 7:30 am The D&R program books have this in their timeline section if it helps: [2014] Rei Ayanami (the 2nd) transfers to the New Tokyo-3 1st Municipal Junior High School.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Apr 30, 2007 2:05 pm Grades in Japan: Middle School/Junior High: 7th, 8th, 9th High School: 10th, 11th, 12th So 7th grade=1st year

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Apr 30, 2007 5:00 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Grades in Japan: Middle School/Junior High: 7th, 8th, 9th High School: 10th, 11th, 12th So 7th grade=1st year

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Apr 30, 2007 7:11 pm so she joined them in 7th grade, they are in 8th grade now, and she has been there like a year?

[]:             Quote Post by Tokpile Quohog » Mon Apr 30, 2007 7:12 pm A little over a year yes.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Apr 30, 2007 12:30 am I'm kind of weak with Japanese, but the only number I can pull out of that sentence is "ichi" or "one", not "nana" or "seven". The ADV version says 7th, but literal translations I've seen says "first". It's a grade level difference.
